item.title: Discovering Descriptors
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
This talk was pre┬Łsent┬Łed at Py┬ŁCon CZ 2017 and Eu┬ŁroPy┬Łthon 2017. Here are the ref┬Łer┬Łences and re┬Łsources for the pre┬Łsen┬Łta┬Łtion.

The code used for the talk, is avail┬Łable here and ┬Łcon┬Łtains some ex┬Łpla┬Łna┬Łtions along with the ex┬Łam┬Łples.

Read moreŌĆ” (1 min remaining to read)

item.title: The __set_name__ method for descriptors
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
De┬Łscrip┬Łtors gen┬Łer┬Łal┬Łly have to in┬Łter┬Łact with at┬Łtributes of the man┬Łaged ob┬Łjec┬Łt, and this is done by in┬Łspect┬Łing __┬Łdic┬Łt__ on that ob┬Łject (or call┬Łing getat┬Łtr/se┬Łtat┬Łtr, but the prob┬Łlem is the same), and find┬Łing the key un┬Łder┬Ł the spe┬Łcif┬Łic name.

For this rea┬Łson, the de┬Łscrip┬Łtor will have to know the name of the key to look ┬Łfor, which is re┬Łlat┬Łed to the name of the at┬Łtribute is man┬Łag┬Łing.

On pre┬Łvi┬Łous ver┬Łsions of Python this had to be done ex┬Łplic┬Łit┬Łly. If we want┬Łed to ┬Łwork around it, there were some more ad┬Łvanced ways to do so. Luck┬Łi┬Łly, af┬Łter PEP-487 (added in Python 3.6), there are some en┬Łhance┬Łments re┬Łgard┬Łing class cre┬Łation, which al┬Łso af┬Łfect┬Łs de┬Łscrip┬Łtors.

Let's re┬Łview the prob┬Łlem, the pre┬Łvi┬Łous ap┬Łproach┬Łes to tack┬Łle it, and the mod┬Łern way of solv┬Łing it.

Read moreŌĆ” (4 min remaining to read)

item.title: Descriptors & Decorators
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
De┬Łscrip┬Łtors are an amaz┬Łing tool to have in our tool┬Łbox, as they come in handy in many op┬Łpor┬Łtu┬Łni┬Łties.

Prob┬Ła┬Łbly the best thing about de┬Łscrip┬Łtors, is that they can im┬Łprove oth┬Łer ┬Łso┬Łlu┬Łtion┬Łs. Let's see how we can write bet┬Łter dec┬Ło┬Łra┬Łtors, by us┬Łing de┬Łscrip┬Łtors.

Read moreŌĆ” (5 min remaining to read)

item.title: Types of Descriptors
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
Re┬Łsum┬Łing from where we left of┬Łf, on the pre┬Łvi┬Łous post, on which we took A first look at de┬Łscrip┬Łtors, it's time to ex┬Łplore their dif┬Łfer┬Łent type┬Łs and how they work in┬Łter┬Łnal┬Łly.

In Python, almost everything is represented with a dictionary. Objects are dictionaries. Classes are objects, hence they also are contained into a dictionary. This is denoted by the __dict__ attribute that objects have.

There are two types of descriptors: data descriptors and non-data ones. If a descriptor implements both [1] __get__() and __set__(), it's called a data descriptor; otherwise is a non-data descriptor.

Read moreŌĆ” (7 min remaining to read)

item.title: A first look at descriptors
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
De┬Łscrip┬Łtors are one of the most pow┬Łer┬Łful fea┬Łtures of Python. The rea┬Łson why they're so pow┬Łer┬Łful is be┬Łcause they en┬Łable us to con┬Łtrol the core op┬Łer┬Ła┬Łtions (get, set, delete) [1], of an at┬Łtribute in a giv┬Łen ob┬Łjec┬Łt, so that we can hook a par┬Łtic┬Łu┬Łlar code, con┬Łtrolled by us, in or┬Łder to mod┬Łi┬Łfy, change, or ex┬Łtend the o┬Łrig┬Łi┬Łnal op┬Łer┬Ła┬Łtion.

Read moreŌĆ” (8 min remaining to read)
